text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in the small town of Hazelworth, there lived a silly owl named Owney. Owney loved nothing more than hanging out at Mrs. Whatsit's old owl tower. It wasn. t just because it was cool. There was an old clock there.OWL stuff. One day, after weeks of dreaming.

Owney figured if he flew often enough, maybe time would drift beside him. So he went berry hunting. First, he came across Elderberry hill. You know—when everything sweeter than honey finds its home there.

There were berries like little bubbles. He took 'em. Then he soared over Cottonwoods Creek. Just saw some tails swimmin'.

Night fell. He saw stars! No rain. He rested. Next morning—ooh!

There! White blossoms, like snow. Hop! Hop! Hop!

He picked some. Dinner.

Afternoon sun. Find myself.

Bookworm! Yes!

That's where I found it
